data.title: Custom Web Design Demand Grows Among California eCommerce Brands, Digital Silk Reports
data.description:
Los Angeles, California--(Newsfile Corp. - August 8, 2025) - Digital Silk, an award-winning agency focused on creating brand strategies, custom websites, and digital marketing campaigns, has noted a rise in custom web design inquiries from California-based eCommerce businesses. The increase reflects broader market interest in personalization, mobile optimization, and speed across retail experiences in 2025.

Custom Web Design Demand Grows Among California eCommerce Brands, Digital Silk Reports

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/10732/261732_57ae59cb9410bab0_001full.jpg

A 2024 Deloitte Digital survey found that 73% of U.S. consumers expect brands to understand their individual needs and expectations. In California's competitive online retail landscape, eCommerce companies are seeking custom web design to address user behavior insights, optimize mobile UX, and enhance visual differentiation from templated platforms.

Design Flexibility Becoming a Competitive Advantage

As plug-and-play website solutions reach market saturation, retailers are exploring custom design to improve functionality, brand recall, and customer retention. According to a 2024 Adobe Commerce Trend Report, the rise in composable and headless commerce is fueling demand for custom development and design capabilities.

Key Features Driving Custom Design Requests

  • Mobile-first UX with fast loading times and responsive layouts
  • Unique homepage and product page templates tailored to user flow
  • Integration of personalization tools and advanced filtering
  • ADA compliance considerations for California retailers
  • Scalable design for multi-brand or DTC expansion

Commentary on Market Shift

"California's eCommerce brands are rethinking templated solutions in favor of purpose-built websites that align with how their customers shop," said Gabriel Shaoolian, CEO of Digital Silk. "Custom web design offers the flexibility to support long-term growth, branding, and cross-device usability."

Digital Silk continues to offer tailored web design and development services to support eCommerce businesses looking to upgrade performance and differentiation in a competitive digital retail market.

About Digital Silk

Digital Silk is an award-winning California Web Design Agency focused on growing brands online. With a team of seasoned experts, Digital Silk creates digital experiences through strategic branding, custom web design, and digital marketing services to help improve visibility and support engagement.

Media Contact

Jessica Erasmus

Marketing Director & PR Manager

Tel: (800) 206-9413

Email: jessica@digitalsilk.com

data.title: Swiss Franc's Resilience to Tariffs Reflects U.S.-Swiss Trade-Deal Hopes — Market Talk
data.description: The Swiss franc's reaction to the U.S. imposing 39% tariffs on Swiss goods has been relatively muted, UBS Global Wealth Management analysts say in a note. "This likely reflects market confidence that a tariff-reducing agreement will eventually be reached." The franc is also not particularly sensitive to domestic growth developments, which remain solid aside from the tariff news, they say. Hopes for lower tariffs along with July's higher-than-expected inflation data and the Swiss National Bank signalling a high hurdle to take rates below the current 0% level mean further rate cuts are unlikely at this stage, they say. The euro trades flat at 0.9405 francs and UBS expects it remain stable in a 0.93-0.94 range until next June. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0817 ET - The Swiss franc's reaction to the U.S. imposing 39% tariffs on Swiss goods has been relatively muted, UBS Global Wealth Management analysts say in a note. "This likely reflects market confidence that a tariff-reducing agreement will eventually be reached." The franc is also not particularly sensitive to domestic growth developments, which remain solid aside from the tariff news, they say. Hopes for lower tariffs along with July's higher-than-expected inflation data and the Swiss National Bank signalling a high hurdle to take rates below the current 0% level mean further rate cuts are unlikely at this stage, they say. The euro trades flat at 0.9405 francs and UBS expects it remain stable in a 0.93-0.94 range until next June. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0718 ET - Germany will likely have to revise down the already weak second-quarter GDP contraction of 0.1% due to industrial data that showed production slumped 1.9% on month in June, Berenberg's Holger Schmieding says. Whereas data had so far indicated that the slippage in 2Q was no more than a minor setback after two quarters of growth, new figures show a more pronounced reversal of fortunes, the analyst says in a note. However, slightly more upbeat surveys for July prompt a small lift to Berenberg's 3Q GDP forecasts from flat to 0.1%. Retail sales grew for a fifth quarter in a row in 2Q, suggesting German consumers are opening their wallets more, and companies also seem to be gearing up to invest more, he adds. (edward.frankl@wsj.com)

0712 ET - U.S. tariffs are massively damaging for the Swiss textile industry and caught everyone off guard, Swiss Textiles association says. The Trump administration implemented tariffs on goods imported from Switzerland of 39%. The country didn't expect this, as experts assumed a worst-case scenario of 31%, the organization says. The duties worsen the position of Swiss groups in relation to competitors from other countries, the trade association says. Last year, the Swiss textile and clothing industry exported goods worth 64 million Swiss francs to the U.S., putting the country in fifth place among the most important markets for the sector. "Swiss Textiles is calling for a continuation of the dialogue with the U.S. and for good relations with other partners such as the EU to be secured," it adds. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

0705 ET - Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller would be the more dollar-supportive choice to become the central bank's next chief compared with some other potential candidates, MUFG Bank's Derek Halpenny says in a note. Waller is emerging as a favorite to replace Fed Chair Jerome Powell, Bloomberg reported. "This should be viewed more positively by the financial markets than say Kevin Hassett who is deemed as very close to President Trump and with less experience." Trump has said Hassett, the director of the National Economic Council, is a frontrunner for the role along with former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh. The DXY dollar index falls 0.1% to 98.319. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0701 ET - Non-U.S. corporate debt remains attractive as an asset class, MFS Investment Management's Owen Murfin says in a note. It carries less risk than the U.S. credit, acting as a possible hedge against rising recession risk, he says. The global credit also provides diversification from increasing fiscal risks in some developed countries like the U.S., U.K., and Japan. The continuing monetary easing by central banks is also likely to boost credit performance, Murfin says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0640 ET - The good performance seen in euro-denominated credit is likely to continue as the eurozone economy is expected to recover, LBBW's Michael Koehler and Benedikt Horwedel say in a note. Euro credit spreads tightened this week on prospects of improved economic performance in the eurozone as governments raise infrastructure spending, the analysts say. "We continue to believe that the economy will stabilize and remain positive about credits." (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0620 ET - The Canadian dollar could fall if upcoming data on the country's labor market are weak, Monex Europe analysts say in a note. A soft employment print would "crack open the door" for the Bank of Canada to resume cutting interest rates in the coming months, they say. This could push the U.S. dollar up to 1.38 Canadian dollars, they say. The U.S. dollar last trades down 0.1% at 1.3726 Canadian dollars. The data are due at 1230 GMT. Economists in a WSJ survey expect the data to show Canada added 10,000 jobs in July, marking a slowdown from 83,100 in June. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0616 ET - The European Central Bank will likely hold off from cutting rates in September as inflation settles at the bank's target, says Oxford Economics' Angel Talavera. Inflation held steady at 2.0% in July, figures showed last week. That will likely keep the ECB's governing council from moving again to lower borrowing costs, having last month held the deposit rate at 2.0%, Talavera says. "Although we still see inflation easing over the next few months, we don't think the progress on inflation will be enough for the council to agree to another cut by then," he writes in a note to investors. The ECB may later in the year cut rates if inflation shows signs of easing, but equally the central bank may decide that to leave them at current levels, Talavera says. (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

0605 ET - Rabobank has a strong capital structure which lowers the possibility of it refinancing the Additional Tier 1 bonds callable in December 2025, ING's Suvi Kosonen says in a note. Additional tier 1 bonds (AT1 bonds) are a type of risky debt issued by banks to meet regulatory capital requirements. The bank targets a common equity tier 1 (CET1) ratio of 14% but is likely to operate at a higher level than this in the medium term, Kosonen says. "We expect the bank to call its AT1 in December 2025 without refinancing," she says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0603 ET - The U.K. economy is likely to have slowed in the three months through June, Investec economist Philip Shaw writes in a note to clients. Figures for Britain's GDP will be set out next week and are likely to show a deceleration to growth of 0.2% for the quarter, down from the strong 0.7% growth booked in the first quarter of the year, according to Investec's estimates. That would give some vindication to the Bank of England's decision this week to lower interest rates by a quarter-point to 4.00%, Shaw says. (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

0559 ET - The Czech koruna rises to a 19-month high against the euro, extending gains after the Czech National Bank left interest rates unchanged Thursday and indicated that policy will remain restrictive. The CNB effectively signalled that the current policy easing cycle has ended, Commerzbank's Tatha Ghose says in a note. The central bank also upgraded its growth and inflation forecasts. These changes back the CNB's guidance and could allow the koruna to modestly extend its outperformance against Central and Eastern European peers, Ghose says. The euro falls to a low of 24.410 korunas, LSEG data show. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0557 ET - The Bank of England is likely nearer to ending interest-rate cuts following this week's tight vote, TwentyFour Asset Management's Johnathan Owen says in a note. The BOE's policymakers voted by five members to four in favor of a 25 basis-point interest-rate cut and highlighted concerns about elevated inflation. Markets have reduced their expectations of BOE rate cuts as a result, currently pricing in only a 70% chance of another rate cut this year, down from a 96% chance priced in prior to the rate decision, LSEG data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: Coffee gains amid low stocks and as tariffs freeze US-Brazil trade
data.description:
Coffee futures on ICE rose on Friday, heading for gains of 7% on the week, as U.S.-Brazil trade remains frozen due to the Trump administration's tariffs, and as stocks continue to slide.

Cocoa and sugar also rose.

COFFEE

* Arabica coffee futures rose 2.6% to $2.9875 per lb at 1159 GMT, having hit a two-week high of $2.9940.

* Dealers said there are next to no new trades between the U.S. and top coffee grower Brazil after Trump imposed a 50% tariff on Brazilian goods.

* As for existing trades, coffee loaded in Brazil by the August 6 tariff deadline can enter the U.S. without paying the tariff, provided it arrives by October 6.

* The U.S. gets about a third of its coffee from Brazil, and market participants are for now drawing on ICE-certified stocks (KC-TOT-TOT), which have fallen to a more than one-year low.

* Hopes the U.S. will exempt Brazilian coffee from tariffs are dimming meanwhile. Brazilian President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva has said he sees no room for direct talks with Trump.

* Robusta coffee rose 2.2% to $3,451 a metric ton.

SUGAR

* Raw sugar ​​​​rose 0.7% to 16.12 cents/lb but was heading for a third consecutive week of losses.

* The UN's Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) said its sugar price index eased for a fifth consecutive month in July on expectations of increased production in Brazil and India, despite signs of recovering sugar import demand.

* Dealers, however, noted ongoing concerns about the ATR (sugar content) in top grower Brazil's cane in 2025/26.

* France's constitutional court on Thursday blocked the re-introduction of a pesticide accused of harming bees.

* White sugar rose 1% to $467.30 a ton.

COCOA

* London cocoa rose 1.5% to 5,488 pounds per ton, heading for gains of 2% this week.

* New York cocoa rose 1.7% to $8,199 a ton.

data.title: Kazakhstan to begin producing its own nuclear fuel
data.description:
Kazakhstan, the world's largest uranium producer, plans to produce its own nuclear fuel for nuclear power plants being built in the Central Asian country in the coming years, its atomic energy agency said on Friday.

Kazakhstan does not yet have its own nuclear power plants, but its uranium reserves, which make up about 15% of the world's total, are second only to Australia’s.

In October, 71.12% of voters in Kazakhstan supported the construction of a nuclear power plant. On Friday, Russia's Rosatom began work on the first nuclear plant, in Kazakhstan's southeastern village of Ulken, local media reported.

The engineering and survey work was started by a subsidiary of Rosatom, and will last at least 18 months, with construction of the plant expected to be completed in 2035. Two more nuclear plants in Kazakhstan are expected to be built by China.

Nuclear power has been promoted by the government of President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev as a way to gradually wean Kazakhstan's growing economy off polluting coal-fired power.

By 2035, Kazakhstan plans to have 2.4 gigawatts of nuclear capacity.

data.title: Wendy's cuts profit outlook as U.S. sales stay weak. Its stock heads for a 5-year low.
data.description:
By Steve Gelsi

CEO Ken Cook says marketing and in-store experiences have to improve to boost U.S. sales, which fell for a second straight quarter while international sales rose

Wendy's stock heads for a 20-month low after an analyst downgrade.

Wendy's Co.'s stock dropped in premarket trading, heading toward a low not seen in five years, as the fast-food burger chain cut its full-year profit and sales outlook, saying its U.S. business continued to weaken.

That U.S. weakness Wendy's (WEN) comes as international sales kept increasing and after rival McDonald's Corp. (MCD) reported strength in the U.S. during the same quarter.

The stock fell 1.8% in premarket trading, putting it on track to open around the lowest prices seen since March 2020.

Wendy's reported overall sales that fell 1.8% from a year ago, as U.S. sales dropped 3.3% to offset an 8.7% jump in international sales. Same-store sales, which are sales at stores open at least 15 months, declined 2.9%, as they declined 3.6% in the U.S. while rising 1.8% overseas.

In the previous quarter, U.S. sales were down 2.6%, while same-restaurant sales fell 2.8%.

Interim Chief Executive Ken Cook said the company has more work to do to improve its U.S. business, including improving marketing programs and customer experiences in the restaurants.

Wendy's now expects 2025 adjusted earnings per share, which excludes nonrecurring items, of 82 cents a share to 89 cents a share, down from its previous estimate of 92 cents a share to 98 cents a share.

Wall Street analysts currently expect 2025 profit of 94 cents a share, according to FactSet data.

The company also cut its sales-growth guidance to a decline of 5% to a decline of 3% from a decline of 2% to flat.

For the second quarter, Wendy's net income rose to $55.1 million, or 29 cents a share, from $54.6 million, or 27 cents a share, in the year-ago quarter. Analysts surveyed by FactSet were looking, on average, for earnings of 25 cents a share.

Revenue fell 1.7% to $560.9 million, but was ahead of the analyst estimate of $558 million.

As of Thursday's close, Wendy's stock has fallen by 38.9% in 2025, while McDonald's shares have gained 6.2% and the S&P 500 index SPX is up by 7.8%.

-Steve Gelsi

data.title: Gold is flying higher - a report of gold-bar tariffs is doing the trick
data.description:
By Barbara Kollmeyer

Swiss gold refiners were already under pressure

Is the Trump administration ready to slap gold bars with a tariff? The industry is on the edge.

Gold futures prices surged toward a record on Friday on a report that the U.S. has ruled that gold bars would be subject to tariffs.

On a continuous basis, gold futures (GC00) rose $24.80, or 0.7%, to $3,478.50 an ounce, after earlier hitting a fresh intraday high of $3,534.20, taking out an April 22 record.

Triggering that jump was a Financial Times report that cited a July 31 letter from U.S. Customs and Border Protection, stating that 1-kilo and 100-ounce gold bars should be classified under a customs code that subjects them to tariffs. MarketWatch has reached out to that agency for comment.

One-kilo bars are commonly traded on Comex, the metals-futures marketplace used for hedging and speculation that sets prices globally for precious metals. They are also the bulk of Switzerland's gold exports to the U.S., and given President Donald Trump recently shocked the country with 39% tariffs, that's the tariff gold is now facing. The industry expected bullion would be exempt from reciprocal tariffs, as the White House had laid out in early April.

If not a mistake, that would mean "serious implications," for the gold market, Commerzbank commodity strategist Carsten Fritsch told clients in a note. "Switzerland is a major supplier of gold bars because it is home to many gold refineries that melt down gold into specific bar sizes."

The country exported 450 tons of gold to the U.S. in the first quarter due to concerns that the precious metal might face future levies. That drove up gold prices on Comex and boosted holdings, he added.

Ole Hansen, head of commodity strategy at Saxo Bank, said the U.S. Comex futures market is "often used by bullion banks globally as a highly liquid, round-the-clock hedging tool for transactions in the physical bullion market." Short positions originally intended as hedges suddenly blew up, he said.

Among those who short gold are gold producers themselves, who try to lock in a profit between what they mine and when it can be sold.

"The decision disrupts the established global trade flow, which traditionally involves large bars moving between London, Switzerland, and the U.S.," Neil Welsh, head of metals at multiasset brokerage Britannia Global Markets, said in a note. Swiss refiners and exporters now face higher costs. If export volumes to the U.S. are reduced, it could increase market uncertainty.

"Several refiners have already responded by halting or reducing shipments amid the confusion over tariff classifications," he said.

Hansen said similar gold-market dislocations were seen during the COVID-19 outbreak when the trans-Atlantic bullion chain stalled. "For now, it's worth watching whether another 'TACO moment' will emerge. If not, the spread may need to settle at a new level that reflects the tariff landscape," he said. TACO is an acronym for Trump Always Chickens Out, a market expression for when the president backs down from a threat.

Hansen and others said Trump's shifting tariff agenda, which also roiled copper in July, may lead some to question whether New York's futures markets can "offer a stable and trustworthy trading environment that offers the best price discovery." Other global centers may also step up as alternatives, he said.

Besides tariffs, gold prices are being supported by prospects for the Federal Reserve easing interest rates and fears of U.S. stagflation, along with continued robust physical demand from China, he said. Gold futures have climbed 31% this year, set to eclipse last year's 27% gain.

As for Switzerland, Simon J. Evenett, professor at IMD Business School in Morges, told MarketWatch that smelting gold is actually a "low value-added commercial activity" in the country.

In the first six months of this year, it shipped $47.5 billion of gold to the U.S., from $12 billion in the second half of 2024 on gold-tariff concerns, with the wealthy moving their holdings from London to New York, he said. The Swiss will probably now shift smelting to the United Arab Emirates, which only faces a 10% U.S. import tariff, he added.

"Gold tariffs at 39% will put a massive dent in this portion of Swiss exports, helping to reduce the artificially inflated Swiss trade surplus with the USA. Lays the groundwork for lower U.S. tariffs down the road," Evenett said.

-Barbara Kollmeyer

data.title: Street View: Eli Lilly stays solid while weight-loss drug stirs debate
data.description:
** Eli Lilly on Thursday raised its full-year profit and sales forecast, betting on surging demand for blockbuster weight-loss drug Zepbound, as it targets new markets and looks to grab more share from Novo Nordisk's Wegovy

** LLY's experimental weight-loss pill helped patients lose 12.4% of body weight in late-stage trial, but fell short of Novo's Wegovy

** Stock rated "buy" on average; median PT is $951 - data compiled by LSEG

MEANINGFUL MOVES IN MEASURED DOSES

** J.P.Morgan ("overweight," PT: $1,100) says LLY is set for further upside, driven by strong demand for Zepbound and Mounjaro, its injectable treatments for obesity and diabetes, and progress on orforglipron, its experimental oral weight-loss pill, adds another potential boost to the story

** Jefferies ("buy," PT: $905) says the bullish outlook for oral obesity drugs is fading and orforglipron shows promise, but safety concerns and side effects raise doubts about its fit for primary care

** Citigroup ("buy," PT: $1,190) sees orforglipron as a strong, approvable product that could boost Lilly's growth. Despite broader biopharma concerns, Lilly stands out for its fundamentals and momentum

** Wells Fargo ("overweight," PT: $1,100) says orforglipron came in softer than expected, but its weight-loss results and safety profile still make it a viable oral option, especially in international markets where pricing can be more flexible

data.title: KALA BIO, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
KALA BIO, Inc., a biopharmaceutical company focused on developing innovative therapies for rare and serious diseases, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and key business developments, highlighting both challenges and progress in its clinical and operational activities.

Financial Highlights

Net Loss: $11.2 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $9.6 million for the same period in 2024, reflecting increased research and development expenses.

Net Loss: $20.1 million for the six months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $21.4 million for the same period in 2024, indicating a slight improvement in cost management.

Net Loss Per Share: $(1.71) for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $(3.16) for the same period in 2024, showing a reduction in loss per share due to an increase in weighted-average shares outstanding.

Net Loss Per Share: $(3.13) for the six months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $(7.32) for the same period in 2024, reflecting improved financial performance per share.

Total Costs and Expenses: $11.0 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $9.6 million for the same period in 2024, primarily due to increased research and development activities.

Total Costs and Expenses: $21.7 million for the six months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $21.5 million for the same period in 2024, indicating stable operational expenses.

Interest Income: $0.4 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $0.5 million for the same period in 2024, reflecting a decrease in interest income.

Interest Expense: $1.1 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $1.5 million for the same period in 2024, showing a reduction in interest expenses.

Grant Income: $0.5 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $1.0 million for the same period in 2024, indicating a decrease in grant income.

Grant Income: $2.9 million for the six months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $2.1 million for the same period in 2024, reflecting an increase in grant income.

Business Highlights

KPI-012 Clinical Development: KPI-012, a mesenchymal stem cell secretome, is in clinical development for treating persistent corneal epithelial defects (PCED). The CHASE Phase 2b clinical trial is underway, with patient enrollment completed in July 2025. Topline safety and efficacy data are expected by the end of September 2025.

Orphan Drug and Fast Track Designations: KPI-012 has received Orphan Drug and Fast Track designations from the FDA for the treatment of PCED, highlighting its potential significance in addressing rare diseases.

Platform Technology Potential: The multifactorial mechanism of KPI-012 suggests it could serve as a platform technology. The company is evaluating its application for other rare front-of-the-eye diseases and has initiated preclinical studies for inherited retinal degenerative diseases.

Alcon Transaction: The company sold the rights to manufacture, sell, and market EYSUVIS and INVELTYS to Alcon in July 2022. This transaction included the AMPPLIFY Drug Delivery Technology and certain liabilities, with potential milestone payments up to $325 million based on sales achievements.

Future Outlook: The company expects to report topline data from the CHASE trial by the end of September 2025. Contingent on positive results, the trial could potentially support a Biologics License Application for KPI-012.

Research and Development Focus: The company is dedicating substantial resources to the development and potential commercialization of KPI-012 for PCED and other indications, including Limbal Stem Cell Deficiency.

Financial Dependency: The company's success depends on developing and commercializing KPI-012, receiving milestone payments from Alcon, and raising additional capital to support operations.

Operational Challenges: The company faces substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern within twelve months due to expected negative operating cash flows and the need for additional funding.

SEC Filing: KALA BIO, Inc. [ KALA ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: Energy Vault Holdings, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Energy Vault Holdings, Inc., a leading provider of energy storage solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter. The report highlights significant financial growth and key operational milestones, reflecting the company's ongoing efforts to expand its market presence and enhance its technological capabilities in the renewable energy sector.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $8.512 million, reflecting a significant increase from $3.770 million in the same period last year, driven by higher sales of energy storage products.
  • Gross Profit: $2.516 million, up from $1.049 million, indicating improved cost management and increased sales volume.
  • Loss from Operations: $(28.148) million, slightly higher than the $(27.885) million loss in the prior year, due to increased operating expenses.
  • Net Loss: $(34.932) million, compared to $(26.199) million, primarily due to higher interest expenses and provision for income taxes.
  • Net Loss Per Share: $(0.22), compared to $(0.18) in the previous year, reflecting the increased net loss.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company recognized revenue from various product and service categories, including the sale of energy storage products, tolling revenue, operation and maintenance services, software licensing, and intellectual property licensing. The sale of energy storage products was the largest contributor, followed by tolling revenue and other services.
  • New Production Launches: During the reporting period, the company placed into service its battery energy storage system in Snyder, Texas, known as the Cross Trails BESS. This marks a significant operational milestone as it transitions from construction in progress to an active energy storage system.
  • Future Outlook: The company is focused on expanding its energy storage solutions and anticipates continued growth in its operational capabilities. Management has highlighted plans to monetize investment tax credits through sales to third-party buyers, which is expected to provide additional financial resources for future operations.
  • Operational Strategy: Energy Vault is transitioning from a build-and-transfer model to taking ownership interests in energy storage assets in select markets. This strategic shift aims to enhance the company's operational footprint and leverage its technology portfolio more effectively.
  • Customer and Market Focus: The company continues to target utilities, independent power producers, and large industrial energy users, emphasizing a solutions-based approach to reduce energy costs and maintain power reliability. This customer-centric strategy is aligned with the global transition to renewable energy sources.

SEC Filing: Energy Vault Holdings, Inc. [ NRGV ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: American Axle & Manufacturing Lifts Sales View Despite 2Q Decline
data.description:
By Dean Seal

American Axle & Manufacturing gave its revenue outlook for 2025 a tweak higher despite seeing the top line slide more than expected in the second quarter.

The auto-parts maker raised the low end of its full-year sales guidance by $100 million to $5.75 billion, and maintained its upper-limit target of $5.95 billion.

For the second quarter, the Detroit company posted a profit of $39.3 million, or 32 cents a share, compared with $18.2 million, or 15 cents a share, in the same quarter a year earlier.

Stripping out one-time items, adjusted earnings were 21 cents a share. Analysts polled by FactSet had been expecting 15 cents a share.

Sales fell 5% to $1.54 billion, coming in below analyst estimates for $1.58 billion, according to FactSet. The top line was hit by lower volumes and weaker product mix.

Write to Dean Seal at dean.seal@wsj.com

data.title: Billy Carson Unveils 4biddenknowledge Academy: Empowering Minds, Fueling Global Impact
data.description:
Billy Carson's 4biddenknowledge Academy Sparks Worldwide Transformation and Company Growth

MIAMI, FL / ACCESS Newswire / August 8, 2025 / Billy Carson Launches 4biddenknowledge Academy: A Global Hub for Quantum Learning, Wealth Building, and Conscious Awakening

Visionary entrepreneur, 5x best-selling author, and founder of 4biddenknowledge Inc., Billy Carson, has officially unveiled the 4biddenknowledge Academy - a revolutionary online learning platform set to transform lives through the integration of spiritual science, financial literacy, ancient wisdom, and quantum consciousness.

Now live at Skool.com/4biddenknowledge, the Academy is not just another digital course hub - it is a global movement for higher learning and self-empowerment. Designed for seekers, achievers, and awakened minds, Carson's Academy delivers high-level training through curated modules such as:

  • Wealth Creation Systems

  • Manifestation Mastery

  • Quantum Health & Longevity

  • Mystery School Teachings

  • Elite Weekly Mentorship

Students are given the tools to rewire their subconscious, unlock hidden potential, and create financial and spiritual freedom - all while connecting with a like-minded community of global change-makers.

"Before founding one of the world's leading conscious media empires, I built multiple 7- and 8-figure businesses," said Carson. "Now, I'm opening the vault to share that blueprint - and combining it with metaphysics and ancient knowledge to truly empower people from the inside out."

The Academy is already driving substantial revenue growth for 4biddenknowledge Inc., quickly emerging as a multi-million-dollar vertical in the company's expanding portfolio. With monthly and annual memberships, live coaching, and exclusive VIP experiences (including luxury giveaways and private sessions worth up to $10,000), the Academy has become a thriving ecosystem of both transformation and profitability.

Yet the greatest measure of success isn't dollars - it's impact.

One student shared, "Thirteen years of fear were abolished in just seven days thanks to the powerful energy and support of this community."

Another, James Garrett, offered this powerful and emotional story:

"After thirteen long years, and with the love and strength of this incredible group, I finally broke through the FEAR of being seen. Fear didn't go quietly - it fought me every single hour for the past seven days. It took me longer to sign this one document than anything else in my life. There were moments I wanted to give up, especially as I tried to rebuild my vision from ground zero.

But I didn't.

Because of the compassion, accountability, and unwavering support of this community, I stood my ground. I refused to back down. And today, I can finally say: I faced that fear and overcame it, right here, with all of you.

What I'm sharing is more than just a photo. It's me. Raw. Unfiltered. Vulnerable. There are no masks, no filters, no hiding. This is who I am, fully visible, and at peace with every part of it.

Thank you all for walking this journey with me. You've helped me step out of the shadows and into the light."

Testimonials like these underscore the Academy's mission: to heal, awaken, and elevate humanity through ancient truths and modern strategy.

As part of its commitment to accessibility, the Academy is offering a limited-time introductory price of just $55/month or $444/year - a 50% discount from its standard $111/month rate. All enrollments include a 7-day risk-free guarantee and access to a vibrant community of high-frequency thinkers, creators, and leaders.

About 4biddenknowledge Inc.

Founded by Billy Carson, 4biddenknowledge Inc. is a global conscious media and education company spanning streaming television, best-selling literature, advanced technology, space exploration, global tours, and now transformative e-learning. Its mission: to bridge the gap between science and spirituality and empower individuals to master their reality through truth, knowledge, and higher consciousness.
